chapter: Chapter: The Amazing World of Solutes, Solvents, and Solutions

questions.question: What is the main difference between a mixture and a solution?
questions.answer: A solution is a uniform mixture, while a mixture may not be uniform.

questions.question: Which component is usually present in larger amount in a solution?
questions.answer: The solvent.

questions.question: Why is salt water considered a homogeneous mixture?
questions.answer: Because salt dissolves uniformly throughout the water.

questions.question: What happens when more solute is added to a saturated solution?
questions.answer: The extra solute will not dissolve.

questions.question: What type of solution can dissolve additional solute?
questions.answer: An unsaturated solution.

questions.question: What is meant by concentration of a solution?
questions.answer: The amount of solute present in a given amount of solvent or solution.

questions.question: Why does heating increase solubility of many solids?
questions.answer: Because heat increases particle movement allowing more solute to dissolve.

questions.question: Why does gas solubility decrease in warm water?
questions.answer: Gas particles escape more easily at higher temperatures.

questions.question: Why is dissolved oxygen important for fish?
questions.answer: Fish use dissolved oxygen in water for respiration.

questions.question: What property determines whether an object floats or sinks?
questions.answer: Density.

questions.question: What happens if an object's density is greater than water?
questions.answer: It sinks in water.

questions.question: What happens if an object's density is lower than water?
questions.answer: It floats on water.

questions.question: Why do ships float even though they are made of heavy metals?
questions.answer: Their overall density is less than that of water.

questions.question: What happens to density when a substance is heated?
questions.answer: Density usually decreases.

questions.question: Why does hot air rise above cold air?
questions.answer: Hot air is less dense than cold air.

questions.question: Why are gases easily compressed?
questions.answer: Because gas particles are far apart.

questions.question: What instrument is commonly used to measure mass in experiments?
questions.answer: A digital weighing balance.

questions.question: What instrument is used to measure volume of liquids accurately?
questions.answer: A measuring cylinder.

questions.question: Why must the reading of a measuring cylinder be taken at eye level?
questions.answer: To avoid parallax error.

questions.question: Which method is used to measure volume of irregular solids?
questions.answer: The water displacement method.

questions.question: Why does ice float on water even though it is made of water?
questions.answer: Ice has lower density than liquid water.

questions.question: What is the purpose of calculating density in experiments?
questions.answer: To identify or compare substances.

questions.question: Why are solutions important in chemistry and daily life?
questions.answer: They allow substances to mix and react uniformly.

questions.question: Why do aquatic animals struggle in polluted warm water?
questions.answer: Because warm water contains less dissolved oxygen.

questions.question: What physical property helps scientists identify unknown liquids?
questions.answer: Density.
